title_en: Summer Game Fest 2026 Live Report — Every Major Reveal, Trailer, Announcement and Update as It Happens
summary_en: Every game, reveal, and surprise from today's Summer Game Fest showcase.
body_en:
It's almost time for Summer Game Fest, Geoff Keighley's annual summer showcase packed with game reveals, new trailers, and fresh updates on upcoming titles. The two-hour livestream kicks off Friday, June 5, at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm UK time, and we'll have live updates below, reporting on everything you need to know as it happens direct from Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles.
We're hoping for a big one this year, following Sony's State of Play earlier this week. But what should we expect to see? Well, there's one game we know will be at Summer Game Fest: Star Wars: Zero Company , the X-COM-inspired take on a galaxy far, far away. A new gameplay trailer is due for that. We also know Guild Wars, ArenaNet's MMORPG series, will be at Summer Game Fest in some capacity. Could we finally see a Guild Wars 3 announcement? Clutch , the new open-world driving game from former Forza Horizon developers, will be at the show, as will Epic Games with a new trailer for the upcoming season of Fortnite . And the newly formed Paramount Games Studio will announce its first game, which, according to Variety , is a AAA title, during Summer Game Fest.
There are all sorts of rumors floating around about other games that may turn up at Summer Game Fest, but one game I'm pretty confident won't be at the show is Grand Theft Auto 6 . Let's be honest, Rockstar Games plays by its own rules. If and when it decides to release a new trailer for the game, it'll do so on its own terms (and probably on some random Tuesday or something).
While we wait for Summer Game Fest to start, let us know in the comments which games you hope to see at the show!
As expected, Star Wars: Zero Company saw its debut trailer revealed at SGF. This X-COM-style Star Wars game is a turn-based strategy affair. The trailer confirmed Anakin Skywalker is in the game! It's due out August 27.
Lords of the Fallen 2 is also coming to Steam this fall. CI Games brought the debut trailer to SGF.
Saw Genesis is a 3v1 multiplayer game based on the Saw horror film franchise.
1666 Amsterdam is the new game from Patrice Desilets, one of the original creators of Assassin's Creed. He took to the stage at SGF to say the prologue is free to download on Steam and the Epic Games Store. 1666: Amsterdam is a dark third-person story-led action-adventure game, with the full release set for early access on PC this year. Console versions are planned for a later date.
Mafia: The Old Country is getting an expansion, and it's coming soon!
RuneScape: Dragonwilds has a new trailer confirming a September 15 release date as well as a release on Xbox Series X and S (it was already confirmed for PlayStation 5 and PC). This is the first time the RuneScape IP has made the jump to consoles in its history.
RGG Studio brought the new Virtua Fighter to SGF, confirming the name of the game: Virtua Fighter Crossroads! The trailer teased a pretty involved and gritty story mode, which seems to revolve around the Chinese mafia. It's due out 2027.
Guild Wars 3! What a huge reveal from ArenaNet. It's due out on PC and PS5 (no mention of Xbox Series X and S. The first beta test is scheduled for fall of 2027.
Former Call of Duty developers revealed a new CrossFire game, backed by Smilegate. It's got dynamic cover, which "fundamentally changes your relationship with the environment, and becomes an active skill you need to master." It's a single-player story driven game. Bit of a surprise this one!
Banda Namco announced Gundam Rogue Orbit with a flashy teaser trailer! It's a fast-paced, high-mobility action game that introduces a brand-new timeline in the Gundam saga, "where humanity must fight for survival against an unknown threat." It's due out in 2027 on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC via Steam.
In Gundam Rogue Orbit, players will suit up as ace pilot RE-X, the protagonist in this new chapter for Gundam. Step into the cockpit of Gundam Helix, RE-X's high-mobility suit, so he along with a rag-tag crew of unsung soldiers, can fight to be humanity’s last hope for survival against this unknown enemy. Gundam Rogue Orbit will feature explosive fast-paced battles set in sprawling environments, with players taking on enemy hordes, massive bosses, and seemingly insurmountable challenges, set against an engaging Sci-Fi backdrop. For Gundam fans, the title promises to deliver the emotionally charged moments and unforgettable action that have made the series one of the world’s most beloved entertainment franchises while also introducing a thrilling new direction for Gundam.
Bayonetta, Nier: Automata, and Ninja Gaiden 4 developer PlatinumGames is working on Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin! Now this is a big reveal. The teaser didn't show us much of anything, but this feels like a match made in heaven. Looking forward to learning more!
Haex is a new game from some of the developers behind The Division. The trailer has a lot of very cool sci-fi things in it, although I'm not entirely sure what was going on. Expect a release in 2027.
RGG Studio brought a new trailer for Stranger Than Heaven, and best of all, Tupac is in it! Yes, Tupac Shakur! What a crazy reveal! And we have a release date: January 15, 2027. Snoop Dog, who is also in the game, talked about Tupac making a surprise appearance in Stranger Than Heaven. Cheers from the crowd! Snoop Dog also said Death Row Games is still alive, and we'll hear more about what it's working on soon.
NetEase brought Blood Message to SGF. Check out the debut trailer, below. It's a cinematic and narrative-driven linear action-adventure currently in development for PC and consoles. Set in the late Tang Dynasty in 848 AD, players take on the role of a nameless messenger who embarks on a perilous eastward journey to deliver a message that holds the fate of his homeland. The game’s setting in the last years of the Tang Dynasty provides a cultural landmark of Chinese history that frames the story with customs, culture, and the rich history of the era, NetEase said.
Fumito Ueda is back! The creator of masterpieces such as Ico and Shadow of the Colossus is working on a sci-fi game. This time, there are enormous robots to climb up. It looks looks fantastic and emotion, as you'd expect.
Creative Assembly showed up at SGF to show a new trailer for Alien: Isolation 2, which looks terrifying indeed. Creative director Al Hope took to the stage to say the developers are creating a game all about escaping a xenomorph. The action is set on a station on a planet now, so you're not locked into a spaceship. Still... scary stuff!
Studio MDHR announced a Cuphead sequel! It's still in the early stages, so we don't know much about it, but we did learn a small team in the studio is working on something completely different, and we got a quick look at it: Mighty Cuphead Adventure! It's a new retro action platformer shooter in the 8-bit style.
The first announcement at SGF is Resident Evil Veronica, a remake of Resident Evil – Code: Veronica. It's due out in 2027! This one had been heavily rumored, but it's great to see it finally officially out in the wild.
And we're off! Keighley is really hyping this one up, saying we've got a great show ahead. Half-Life 3 confirmed?
Right! Just over 20 minutes until Summer Game Fest gets underway! Geoff Keighley has tweeted to say today's final announcement will be a single-player, narrative game, which seems like a decision made in response to the fallout from Highguard's ill-fated reveal at The Game Awards 2025. "Thank you for the feedback!" he said. But what could it be? Half-Life 3, right? RIGHT?!
Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.
